items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. The city also shared updates on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, outlining improvements to library services and physical spaces.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street project hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 with no new hearing date scheduled yet. Residents awaiting details on this development will need to watch for a future notice.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue gets traffic signal upgrade at railroad crossing
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to upgrade traffic signals where Bascom Avenue crosses railroad tracks, part of an ongoing highway-rail safety improvement project. This aims to improve safety and traffic flow at a key intersection.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand arts and cultural programs at the facility.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised criteria for evaluating projects. This plan will shape biking and walking infrastructure investments across the city.

items.headline: Council Vacancy Process Changed, Reduces Appointment Steps
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, streamlining the process by reducing steps. This could affect how quickly vacant council seats are filled in the future.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a major $8.8M senior housing development with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's shortage of dedicated senior living options. This is one of the larger multi-family projects in this permit batch.

items.headline: New housing cluster approved on Gay Avenue and Capitol
items.summary: Multiple new residential units approved across nearby lots (duplex, three single-family homes) totaling ~$1.6M in construction value, indicating infill development in this neighborhood corridor.
